Taiyo Tono.bio: Taiyo Tono is a Japanese painter based in Kyoto, born in Osaka in 1986. She studied architecture and design at Kyoto Seika University's Faculty of Art, an education that instilled in her a precise sense of structure and space - qualities that now underpin her expressive, color-saturated paintings. Her work draws from travel, nature, and the emotional texture of everyday life - botanical forms, coastal light, mountain snow, and quiet urban scenes rendered with a vivid, joyful palette. Each painting carries a sense of warmth and movement that has resonated with collectors across the United States, Europe, and Asia. Tono has participated in 19 exhibitions and her works have sold for up to $4,300 on Artsy. She continues to expand her practice through international collaboration and travel, with a particular interest in the natural landscapes and living cultures of Asia.

Yuko Yamazaki.bio: Yuko Yamazaki is a Japanese painter and picture book author based between Kanagawa and Hokkaido. Since her debut as a children's book illustrator in 1999 - with publishers including Shikousha, Shogakukan, and Suzuki Shuppan - her work has been rooted in lived experience: stories felt before they are told. Encouraged by Takeshi Matsumoto of the Chihiro Art Museum, she began developing a parallel practice in large-scale painting around 2015, expanding her quiet narrative sensibility onto canvas. Yamazaki paints not from spectacle but from reverence. She observes wind, listens to light, and gathers impressions of the natural world as they are felt. Her process resembles keeping a diary of the heart: searching for lines, layering colors, recording subtle shifts of feeling. A path between trees becomes an invitation to serenity; a family at dawn becomes a moment suspended in shared breath. Colors merge softly, and space unfolds with patience. Time spent in Hokkaido - her second home - has deepened this engagement, sharpening her sensitivity to seasonal change, clarity of air, and the particular quality of northern light. Her work has been exhibited internationally at the Busan Annual Market of Art (BAMA) from 2020 to 2024, and at Hanshin Department Store Umeda in Osaka from 2022 to 2023, with sales at both. She holds near-annual solo exhibitions at Totora-do gallery in Zushi, Kanagawa, and has created corporate calendar illustrations for Seicomart (Hokkaido, 2023-2024) and Taki Kagaku (Hyogo, 2025-2026). More than ten works have sold on Artsy, with prices reaching up to $1,600. To live with a Yamazaki painting is to be reminded that the world is already gentle.

Alex Breaux.bio: Alex Breaux is a New Orleans-born painter based in Miami whose work is shaped by memory, displacement, and the cultural pull between those two cities. In a 2024 interview, she describes Hurricane Katrina and her family's move to Miami as formative experiences, notes that she earned her BA in Art from Florida International University in 2020, and says she continued graduate study in painting there. Working primarily in oil, she builds narrative paintings around childhood recollection, New Orleans traditions, and the overlooked objects and simple pleasures of everyday life.

Alix P. White.bio: Listed on her official site as Alix White, she appears to be the same painter presented in sales records as Alix P. White. White is a landscape painter who has focused for more than 30 years on the South Shore of Massachusetts and the Thousand Islands region of New York and Ontario, beginning works on location and developing them further in the studio. Her CV notes study in Boston University's School of Fine and Applied Arts core program and completion of a BFA in painting at California College of Arts and Crafts; across her site, she frames her practice around nature, stewardship, and healing.

Alojzij Kogov\u0161ek.bio: Alojzij Kogovsek (1909-1984) was a Slovenian sculptor documented as the artist behind the Miner monument created between 1948 and 1952. The near life-size bronze figure, made in versions for Ljubljana and Velenje, is described as one of the more important sculptures produced soon after liberation and as a work grounded in realism while responding to socialist realism. His practice belongs to the postwar Slovenian and Yugoslav public-art context, with a strong emphasis on monumentality and figurative form.

Enrico Donati.bio: Enrico Donati (1909-2008) was an Italian-born American painter and sculptor born in Milan who first studied economics, then avant-garde music, and later trained in painting in Paris. After moving to New York in 1939, he was embraced by Andre Breton and entered the Surrealist circle around figures such as Marcel Duchamp, Max Ernst, and Yves Tanguy. Over a long career, Donati developed major bodies of work including the Moonscapes and Fossil series, building a distinctive language of texture, color, alchemy, and enigmatic imagery.

Marina Fisher.bio: Marina Fisher is a painter based in Phuket, Thailand, whose work blends contemporary and impressionist approaches to cityscapes, florals, gardens, and seascapes. Her profile describes a practice centered on original paintings that often pair Parisian imagery, especially the Eiffel Tower, with vibrant flowers and natural motifs, using both small acrylic works on paper and richly textured impasto oils on canvas or cardboard. The result is a poetic dialogue between architecture and nature, structure and spontaneity, aimed at viewers drawn to travel, memory, and everyday beauty.

Mia Farrington.bio: Mia Farrington is a Portland, Oregon-based painter born in 1979 whose elliptical pools of color are influenced by the ponds, lakes, and quarries that surrounded her in rural Vermont. Her work is noted for carefully calibrated palettes and edge relationships that turn color into a vehicle for perception and introspection. Recent exhibition history includes work shown at Hashimoto Contemporary and the solo exhibition Parallels at Stephanie Chefas Projects.

qiyi ma.bio: qiyi ma is a Chinese painter born in 1992 in Chongqing who studied at the Accademia di Belle Arti di Brera and in Rome. Her gallery biography describes a practice shaped by Buddhist philosophy and by a period of mountain isolation undertaken to deepen meditation, an experience that became central to her artistic research. Her paintings invite viewers to step away from daily frenzy and seek inward calm, often using windows, openings, and partitions as motifs for looking beyond visible surfaces.

Thomas Geyer.bio: Thomas Geyer is a Leipzig painter born in 1980 who studied painting and graphic design in Leipzig and later worked with Sighard Gille and Annette Schroter; he lives and works in Leipzig. His paintings draw motifs from the countryside around his home region and grow from preliminary sketches that merge real and invented places. Sparse in human presence and rich in fairy-tale and mythic atmosphere, his images return viewers to nature, memory, and the uncanny.
